_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
source_files: Surgery for Artery Anuerysm-Renal Artery PPD_CPD.pdf
min_doctor_qualification: MCh/ or equivalent (in Cardiothoracic Surgery, Vascular Surgery)
clinical_indications: Symptomatic renal artery aneurysm - Hypertension (from associated renal artery stenosis, refractory to medical management), pain, renal ischemia or infarction secondary to embolization from the aneurysm sac
Renal artery aneurysms in females who are pregnant or are contemplating pregnancy.
Renal artery aneurysm associated with acute dissection.

preauth.label: Clinical notes with indication and admission notes.
Relevant investigations - Angiogram / CT Angiogram / MRI reports

claim.label: Is the barcode of the graft if used submitted? (optional)

common_queries: Patient's clinical notes and investigation reports (Angiogram / CT / MRI) were not suggestive for the procedure.
Clinical notes with indication and admission notes not submitted at pre-authorization.
Relevant investigations (Angiogram / CT Angiogram / MRI reports) not submitted at pre-authorization.
Detailed indoor case papers (ICPs) not submitted at claim submission.
Detailed procedure/operative notes not submitted at claim submission.
Detailed discharge summary not submitted at claim submission.
Clinical indication threshold of 'Diameter greater than 2 cm' for renal artery aneurysm not met or documented.

clinical_key_pointers: A renal artery aneurysm (RAA) is defined as a dilated segment of renal artery that exceeds twice the diameter of a normal renal artery.
Symptomatic RAAs can cause hypertension, pain, hematuria, and renal infarction.
Asymptomatic RAAs may seem benign, but the potential for rupture and fistulization increases with size.
Asymptomatic patients can be referred for elective repair, but if patients are symptomatic, further investigation with possible surgical intervention should be considered.
surgical_options: Arteriorrhaphy with or without patch arteriography
Nephrectomy which is usually an unplanned event necessitated by complications such as aneurysm rupture or end-stage ischemic nephropathy.
